content.problemStatement:
There are NNN cities on a circle, numbered 111 through NNN. For each iii (1≤i≤N−11 \le i \le N-11≤i≤N−1), cities iii and i+1i+1i+1 are directly connected by a bidirectional road with length AiA_iAi​, and cities NNN and 111 are also directly connected by a bidirectional road with length ANA_NAN​. However, we do not know the lengths of some roads.

For each city iii, we do know that it has an opposite city — formally, there is a city j≠ij \neq ij=i such that the clockwise distance between cities iii and jjj is equal to the counterclockwise distance between these cities.

Please find the lengths of all roads in such a way that the above condition is satisfied and the sum of lengths of all roads is minimised.
constraints: 1≤Ai≤1091 \le A_i \le 10^91≤Ai​≤109 or Ai=−1A_i = -1Ai​=−1 for each valid iii
the sum of NNN for all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105
**Subtask #2 (20 points):** Ai=±1A_i = \pm 1Ai​=±1 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. For each valid iii, Ai=−1A_i = -1Ai​=−1 denotes that the length of road iii is unknown.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a line containing the string `"NO"` if there is no solution or `"YES"` otherwise. If a solution exists, print a second line containing NNN space-separated positive integers — the lengths of all roads in your solution. Each of these integers should be ≤109\le 10^9≤109. If there are multiple solutions, you may print any one.
